extracted_information: This document provides a detailed comparison between CRDTs (Conflict-free Replicated Data Types) and OT (Operational Transformation) algorithms for data synchronization and conflict resolution. It covers their algorithmic specifics, complexity, performance characteristics, ease of use, and suitability for various use cases, particularly focusing on collaborative and offline-first applications, and text synchronization challenges like the interleaving anomaly.

CRDTs.upstream_update_process: Transform local operations into CRDT's data structure
CRDTs.interleaving_anomaly_prevalence: Weaker forms in libraries like Automerge; Loro (Fugue algorithm) avoids it entirely.
CRDTs.buffering: Operation-based CRDTs need local operations buffer (e.g., Automerge compresses it).

OT.interleaving_anomaly_prevalence: Strong forms in popular OTs (Jupiter, adOPTED, SOCT2).
OT.buffering: Clients must buffer all operations until synchronized.

features.description: Both CRDTs and OT solve data conflicts (concurrent writes). Avoiding conflicts (single owner, partitioning, single-leader replication) is preferred if possible. Simple overwriting strategies (LWW, node hierarchies) lead to data loss.

features.CRDTs: Easier to use, larger ecosystem, more support, more built-in integrations. Recommended for applications without special requirements.
features.OT: Smaller ecosystem, less support, fewer built-in integrations, minimal algorithms are complicated and unintuitive.

features.CRDTs: Better for distributed synchronization and scenarios where 'n' (amount of operations) can grow large, such as applications with offline support (PWAs).
features.OT: Complexity ($O(n)$ or $O(n^2)$) is less of an issue in real-time synchronization where 'n' is small. Can be a concern when 'n' grows large (e.g., prolonged offline periods).

features.CRDTs: Typically faster at *downstream* updates (synchronizing changes with other users). Upstream responsiveness involves transforming local operations.
features.OT: Faster at *upstream* updates ($O(1)$ immediate local application). This is more noticeable and generally preferred by users. All transformations happen elsewhere.

features.CRDTs: Weaker forms of anomaly. Loro (Fugue algorithm) avoids it entirely, making CRDTs a clear winner in this area.
features.OT: Strong forms of anomaly can appear in popular OTs (Jupiter, adOPTED, SOCT2).

references.title: A great paper in favor of OTs
references.note: Dispels myths about OT often made by proponents of CRDTs.

references.title: Performance comparison of OTs and CRDTs
Interleaving anomaly in CRDTs and OT algorithms
Evaluating CRDTs for Real-time Document Editing
references.note: For complexity analysis of CRDT algorithms.

references.title: Large-Scale Geo-Replicated Conflict-free Replicated Data Types
references.note: About local operations buffer in operation-based CRDTs.
